questions.q: In which year was the actress Smita Patil born?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil was born on 17 October 1955.

questions.q: In which year did the actress Smita Patil die?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil died on 13 December 1986 at the age of 31.

questions.q: Smita Patil is most closely associated with which movement in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil was one of the leading faces of India's Parallel cinema movement.

questions.q: Which 1976 film, directed by Shyam Benegal, featured Smita Patil in a milk-cooperative story set in rural Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil starred in Shyam Benegal's Manthan (1976), inspired by the Gujarat dairy cooperative movement.

questions.q: How many National Film Awards for Best Actress did Smita Patil win during her career?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil won two National Film Awards for Best Actress, for Bhumika (1977) and Chakra (1981).

questions.q: Which civilian honour was awarded to Smita Patil by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil was awarded the Padma Shri in 1985.

questions.q: In the 1977 film Bhumika, Smita Patil played a character based on the life of which kind of figure?
questions.explanation: In Bhumika (1977) Smita Patil played an actress, in a story inspired by the memoirs of Marathi actress Hansa Wadkar.

questions.q: Smita Patil's son, also an actor in Hindi cinema, is named what?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil's son with Raj Babbar is the actor Prateik Babbar.

questions.q: Before becoming a film actress, in what role did Smita Patil first appear on Doordarshan?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil worked as a television newsreader on Bombay Doordarshan before entering films.

questions.q: Which 1987 film, released after her death, featured Smita Patil resisting a tyrannical subedar at a spice factory?
questions.explanation: Mirch Masala (1987), directed by Ketan Mehta, featured Smita Patil in one of her most acclaimed roles, released after her death.

questions.q: Which director gave Smita Patil her early breakthrough roles in films like Nishant and Manthan?
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal cast Smita Patil in landmark early films including Nishant (1975) and Manthan (1976).

questions.q: For which 1977 film did Smita Patil win her first National Film Award for Best Actress?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil won her first National Film Award for Best Actress for Bhumika (1977).

questions.q: For which 1981 film, set in a Mumbai slum, did Smita Patil win her second National Film Award for Best Actress?
options: Albert Pinto Ko Gussa Kyoon Aata Hai
questions.explanation: Smita Patil won her second National Film Award for Best Actress for Chakra (1981), set in a Bombay slum.

questions.q: In which Maharashtra city did Smita Patil grow up and spend her early life?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil grew up and studied in Pune, Maharashtra.

questions.q: Smita Patil's father, Shivajirao Patil, was prominent in which field?
questions.explanation: Her father, Shivajirao Girdhar Patil, was a Maharashtra politician and state minister.

questions.q: Which 1982 film, directed by Mahesh Bhatt, starred Smita Patil alongside Shabana Azmi in a story about an extramarital affair?
questions.explanation: Arth (1982), directed by Mahesh Bhatt, featured Smita Patil opposite Shabana Azmi and Kulbhushan Kharbanda.

questions.q: Smita Patil co-starred with which actor frequently in Parallel cinema, including in Bhumika and Mandi?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil and Naseeruddin Shah were frequent Parallel-cinema co-stars, appearing together in films such as Bhumika and Mandi.

questions.q: Which 1983 Shyam Benegal film, set in a brothel, featured Smita Patil alongside Shabana Azmi?
questions.explanation: Mandi (1983), directed by Shyam Benegal, featured Smita Patil and Shabana Azmi in a satire set in a brothel.

questions.q: Besides art-house films, Smita Patil also acted in commercial Hindi cinema opposite which superstar in films like Namak Halaal and Shakti?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil appeared opposite Amitabh Bachchan in mainstream hits including Namak Halaal (1982) and Shakti (1982).

questions.q: Who directed the 1980 film Aakrosh, in which Smita Patil appeared in a story of tribal injustice?
questions.explanation: Aakrosh (1980) was the directorial debut of Govind Nihalani and featured Smita Patil.

questions.q: Whom did Smita Patil marry, a fellow actor and later politician?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil married actor and politician Raj Babbar.

questions.q: At what age did Smita Patil die in 1986?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil died at the age of 31, shortly after giving birth to her son.

questions.q: Smita Patil died shortly after which personal event in 1986?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil died of childbirth-related complications about two weeks after the birth of her son Prateik.

questions.q: Which 1982 film, directed by Sagar Sarhadi, featured Smita Patil in a story about the buying and selling of young brides in Hyderabad?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil starred in Bazaar (1982), Sagar Sarhadi's film about the trade in poor Hyderabadi brides, alongside Naseeruddin Shah and Farooq Shaikh.

questions.q: Besides Mirch Masala, Smita Patil worked with director Ketan Mehta on which earlier film?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil appeared in Ketan Mehta's debut feature Bhavni Bhavai (1980) before their later collaboration on Mirch Masala (1987).

questions.q: In Ketan Mehta's Mirch Masala, what product is processed at the factory where Smita Patil's character takes refuge?
questions.explanation: Mirch Masala is set at a red-chilli and spice factory, where the women make a final stand against the subedar.

questions.q: Which 1977 Marathi film, directed by Jabbar Patel, featured Smita Patil opposite Mohan Agashe in a story set among a tribal community?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil starred in the Marathi film Jait Re Jait (1977), directed by Jabbar Patel, opposite Mohan Agashe.

questions.q: Smita Patil starred in the 1982 Marathi film Umbartha, directed by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Umbartha (1982), about a woman running a welfare home, was directed by Jabbar Patel.

questions.q: In Shyam Benegal's Manthan, Smita Patil played a woman from which marginalized community in the village?
questions.explanation: In Manthan, Smita Patil played Bindu, a spirited woman from the village's Harijan (Dalit) community.

questions.q: Smita Patil appeared in the 1985 multi-starrer Ghulami opposite which leading actor?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil appeared in Ghulami (1985), a J. P. Dutta film featuring Dharmendra in the lead.
